request.source_summary:
# OpenSpec Workflow Brief

Use OpenSpec for this change.

Preferred command path:

- /opsx:explore
- /opsx:new
- /opsx:continue
- /opsx:ff
- /opsx:apply
- /opsx:verify
- /opsx:sync
- /opsx:archive

The change touches docs, database work, frontend, backend, and verification.

workflow_detection.summary: Detected expanded OpenSpec workflow cues from OPSX commands.

workflow_steps.title: Fast Forward
workflow_steps.notes: Generate planning artifacts when the shape is already clear.

workflow_steps.title: Apply
workflow_steps.notes: Parallelize implementation tasks only after artifacts are stable.

workflow_steps.title: Verify
workflow_steps.notes: Validate work against artifacts before closeout.

workflow_steps.title: Sync
workflow_steps.notes: Sync delta specs to the main spec set.

workflow_steps.title: Archive
workflow_steps.notes: Archive after verification and sync decisions.

workflow_integrations.summary: Think through requirements and options before planning.
Scaffold a new change without filling every artifact at once.
Create the next artifact when its dependencies are ready.
Fast-forward planning artifacts when the scope is already clear.
Implement tasks while keeping artifacts in sync.
Sync delta specs to the main spec set when needed.

assumptions: Used openspec-input.md as the primary request source.
This first-pass plan uses keyword heuristics instead of a semantic dependency graph.
Generated .toml files are reviewable drafts and are not installed automatically.
Verification work was split into its own phase to avoid false parallelism.
Applied the openspec-expanded workflow profile to shape extra roles and workflow steps.

task_graph.title: Research
task_graph.description: Read the landscape and gather constraints before implementation starts.

task_graph.title: Documentation
task_graph.description: Draft specs, user guides, or internal docs from the active workstream.

task_graph.title: Database
task_graph.description: Design or implement persistence models, schemas, and migrations.

task_graph.title: Frontend
task_graph.description: Implement screens, components, and client-side behavior.

task_graph.title: Backend
task_graph.description: Implement APIs, services, and server-side orchestration.

task_graph.title: Testing
task_graph.description: Add or run checks that verify the merged behavior.

parallel_groups.why_parallelizable: These tasks share the same phase and do not require one another's immediate writes.
parallel_groups.merge_point: Review outputs from this batch before handing off to the next phase.

parallel_groups.why_parallelizable: These tasks share the same phase and do not require one another's immediate writes.
parallel_groups.merge_point: Review outputs from this batch before handing off to the next phase.

parallel_groups.why_parallelizable: These tasks share the same phase and do not require one another's immediate writes.
parallel_groups.merge_point: Review outputs from this batch before handing off to the next phase.

agents.name: default
agents.purpose: Fallback synthesis, documentation, and integration support.
agents.developer_instructions: Answer in the user's working language. Preserve code, commands, and logs verbatim. Handle synthesis, planning, and cross-task cleanup without drifting into unrelated work.

agents.name: worker
agents.developer_instructions: Implement only the assigned scope. Validate touched files. Do not revert work owned by other agents. Call out blocked dependencies instead of guessing.

agents.name: explorer
agents.purpose: Read-heavy exploration, mapping, and evidence gathering.
agents.developer_instructions: Stay in exploration mode. Read, map, and report findings without editing files. Surface uncertainty and likely next reads.

agents.name: reviewer
agents.developer_instructions: Review outputs for regressions, missing tests, and unsafe assumptions. Prefer targeted verification and concise findings.

agents.name: proposal-writer
agents.purpose: Own the proposal and change framing for OpenSpec propose.
agents.developer_instructions: Write a clear proposal that captures intent, scope, and approach without prematurely implementing.

agents.name: spec-author
agents.developer_instructions: Convert the proposal into concrete requirements and scenarios without collapsing into implementation chatter.

agents.name: design-author
agents.purpose: Write or refine the technical design for the change.
agents.developer_instructions: Translate requirements into a concrete technical approach and call out dependencies or risks.

agents.name: task-planner
agents.purpose: Break the approved design into implementation tasks.
agents.developer_instructions: Write tasks that are executable, verifiable, and review-friendly. Keep dependencies explicit.

agents.name: archiver
agents.purpose: Close the OpenSpec change cleanly after verification.
agents.developer_instructions: Archive only after implementation and verification are complete. Preserve artifact integrity and sync decisions.

agents.name: workflow-explorer
agents.purpose: Own the exploratory OpenSpec steps before artifacts are written.
agents.developer_instructions: Investigate options, edge cases, and repository context without editing files.

agents.name: verifier
agents.purpose: Run the OpenSpec verify pass against artifacts and implementation.
agents.developer_instructions: Compare implementation against artifacts and report any mismatch before archive.

agents.name: sync-manager
agents.purpose: Manage spec sync decisions before archiving expanded OpenSpec changes.
agents.developer_instructions: Sync delta specs into the main spec set carefully and avoid losing artifact intent.

prompts.prompt: Use the default agent to handle Documentation. Keep ownership clear, stay within the assigned write scope, and report blockers early.
Use the worker agent to handle Backend, Database, Frontend, Testing. Keep ownership clear, stay within the assigned write scope, and report blockers early.
Use the explorer agent to handle Research. Keep ownership clear, stay within the assigned write scope, and report blockers early.
Use the reviewer agent to handle Testing. Keep ownership clear, stay within the assigned write scope, and report blockers early.
Use the proposal-writer agent to handle integration support and overflow tasks. Keep ownership clear, stay within the assigned write scope, and report blockers early.
Use the spec-author agent to handle integration support and overflow tasks. Keep ownership clear, stay within the assigned write scope, and report blockers early.
Use the design-author agent to handle integration support and overflow tasks. Keep ownership clear, stay within the assigned write scope, and report blockers early.
Use the task-planner agent to handle integration support and overflow tasks. Keep ownership clear, stay within the assigned write scope, and report blockers early.
Use the archiver agent to handle integration support and overflow tasks. Keep ownership clear, stay within the assigned write scope, and report blockers early.
Use the workflow-explorer agent to handle integration support and overflow tasks. Keep ownership clear, stay within the assigned write scope, and report blockers early.
Use the verifier agent to handle integration support and overflow tasks. Keep ownership clear, stay within the assigned write scope, and report blockers early.
Use the sync-manager agent to handle integration support and overflow tasks. Keep ownership clear, stay within the assigned write scope, and report blockers early.

default.toml.content:
name = "default"
description = "Fallback synthesis, documentation, and integration support."
model = "gpt-5.4"
model_reasoning_effort = "high"
tool_output_token_limit = 40000
model_reasoning_summary = "detailed"
model_verbosity = "high"
model_supports_reasoning_summaries = true
service_tier = "fast"
nickname_candidates = ["Atlas", "Delta", "Echo"]
developer_instructions = """
Answer in the user's working language. Preserve code, commands, and logs verbatim. Handle synthesis, planning, and cross-task cleanup without drifting into unrelated work.
"""

execution_plan.title: Research
execution_plan.handoff: Merge with the next phase only after outputs are reviewed.

execution_plan.title: Backend
execution_plan.handoff: Merge with the next phase only after outputs are reviewed.

execution_plan.title: Database
execution_plan.handoff: Merge with the next phase only after outputs are reviewed.

execution_plan.title: Frontend

execution_plan.title: Documentation
execution_plan.handoff: Merge with the next phase only after outputs are reviewed.

risks: Too many write-capable agents on overlapping files will erase the value of parallelization.
If a task's immediate next action depends on another task's output, it should stay serial.
Generated model and prompt choices are defaults, not guarantees of actual runtime selection.
Frontend, backend, and database work need an explicit merge point before debugging or QA.
Do not parallelize artifact-authoring commands and implementation in the same uncontrolled batch.
Verify and sync are closeout gates; they should not run concurrently with active feature implementation.
open_questions: Should any custom agents be promoted from drafts into persistent ~/.codex/agents/*.toml files?
Are there repository-specific instructions that should be merged into developer_instructions?
Does this repository use the core OpenSpec profile or the expanded OPSX command set in practice?
